title: Reconcile unowned post-merge Bureau dirty state without data loss
goal: Determine the origin and validity of the post-merge edits that appeared in the protected T046 closeout worktree after PR #517 merged, preserve any legitimate evidence, reject claims contradicted by authoritative live runtime state, and only then clean the worktree and release its retained leases.

acceptance.assertion: The complete dirty diff, file hashes, timestamps, branch head, leases and any available audit or process evidence are captured before any modification, reset, branch deletion or worktree removal.
The likely writer or execution path is identified where evidence permits; unknown provenance remains explicit rather than being attributed by assumption.
Each proposed evidence correction is checked against authoritative live runtime, GitHub and Bureau state; claims contradicted by live evidence are not published.
Any legitimate correction is published through a fresh clean branch and normal review gate, not by silently committing the protected dirty worktree.

After valid information is preserved or published, the protected worktree and branch are removed only with explicit clean-or-preserved evidence and the four retained owner leases are released with readback.

does_not_establish: that the dirty edits are malicious
that the dirty edits belong to the T046 operator
permission to reset or delete the dirty worktree
that the proposed runtime-head correction is true

origin_assessment.result: The protected worktree later became clean at the exact PR #518 head and branch, strongly linking the transient edits to that parallel publication path.
does_not_claim: a specific human or agent writer

truth_reconciliation.contradicted_claim: PR #518 recorded 7e3700938fbed3fc192d1528b630587c5b17ec76 as the deployed runtime head.
truth_reconciliation.resolution: Corrected through a fresh clean branch and normal review gate; no protected dirty-state content was silently committed.

cleanup.readback: No T046 closeout worktree or protected T046/T049 lease remains under the original owner.
